summary.intent: Implement session tab persistence so open tabs (session detail, branch log, spawn) survive app quit and reopen.
summary.outcome: Tab persistence via localStorage was already partially implemented in uncommitted changes; the assistant identified that homePathname was not persisted and began completing the implementation across multiple renderer files.

repo: Tab types include 'home', 'session', 'branchLog', and 'spawn' (renamed from 'launcher'); spawn tabs carry a spawnedAt timestamp
Sessions visible only via LogEvent hook writes (not yet indexed) are now included in the server-side session list
WebSocket poll interval was reduced from 2s to 500ms for faster session detection
SessionDetail uses a 500ms fast-poll when a session is live and supports a hideTerminal flag for use inside SpawnSessionScreen
EmbeddedTerminal accepts a command prop that is typed into the shell 300ms after WS connects
ActiveSessions.handleSpawn opens a spawn tab directly instead of calling the spawnSession() API

code.finding: Tab persistence uses localStorage; session and branchLog tabs survive reload but homePathname (current nav path in home tab) was not being persisted, causing home tab to reset to '/' on restart
New screen showing split view: terminal on bottom, session detail auto-appears above once new session is detected
command prop added; types command into shell 300ms after WS connects
Sessions list now includes hook-written LogEvent sessions not yet indexed by the main indexer

workflow: In Electron, localStorage persists across app restarts without additional configuration
Resuming a session requires reading both git status and recent diffs to reconstruct in-progress state before continuing

friction: homePathname persistence was overlooked in the initial localStorage implementation, requiring a follow-up fix
The session listing had a gap where hook-written sessions were invisible until the server was updated to include LogEvent-visible sessions
open_items: Verify homePathname is now correctly persisted and restored on app relaunch
SessionRow onResume button was removed — confirm no other surfaces relied on it
Dev mode port instability was flagged as a potential issue but not investigated
The 500ms WS poll and fast-poll in SessionDetail may need tuning for performance at scale
